data.content:
### Pty Constructor and Creation

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.3/commands/page.mdx

Initializes the Pty module for pseudo-terminal interactions and provides a method to create new PTY instances. The creation process requires specific options and returns a CommandHandle for managing the PTY. Dependencies include Transport, ConnectionConfig, and PtyCreateOpts.

```typescript
new Pty(transport: Transport, connectionConfig: ConnectionConfig): Pty
create(opts: PtyCreateOpts): Promise<CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Set Command User - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.7/commands/page.mdx

Specifies the username under which the command should be executed. Defaults to the user who started the command.

```typescript
optional user: Username;
```

--------------------------------

### Run TypeScript code with Axios in E2B

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/code-interpreting/supported-languages/javascript/page.mdx

This snippet demonstrates how to run TypeScript code that utilizes the `axios` library to fetch data from an API. It shows the process of creating a sandbox, installing external packages like `axios` using `npm install`, and then executing the TypeScript code. The example includes handling of top-level await and ESM-style imports.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from "@e2b/code-interpreter";

// Create a new sandbox
const sbx = await Sandbox.create();

// Install the axios package
await sbx.commands.run("npm install axios");

// Run the code
const execution = await sbx.runCode(`
  import axios from "axios";

  const url: string = "https://api.github.com/status";
  const response = await axios.get(url);
  response.data;
`,
  { language: "ts" }
);

console.log(execution);
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

# Create a new sandbox
sbx = Sandbox.create()

# Install the axios package
sbx.commands.run("npm install axios")

#  Run the code
execution = sbx.run_code("""
  import axios from "axios";

  const url: string = "https://api.github.com/status";
  const response = await axios.get(url);
  response.data;
""",
    language="ts",
)

print(execution)
```

--------------------------------

### Create New PTY (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

Creates a new pseudo-terminal (PTY) session within the sandbox environment. It accepts creation options and returns a `CommandHandle` for interacting with the newly created PTY.

```typescript
create(opts: PtyCreateOpts): Promise<CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Convert Execution Result to JSON in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-js-sdk/v1.5.0/messaging/page.mdx

Serializes the `Execution` object into a plain JavaScript object for easier data handling and transmission. The resulting object contains the error, logs, and results of the execution.

```typescript
toJSON(): object
```

--------------------------------

### Pty Constructor and Methods - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.1.1/commands/page.mdx

Initializes the Pty module for interacting with pseudo-terminals. It includes methods to create new PTYs, kill existing ones, and resize them. This module is essential for managing interactive terminal sessions within the sandbox.

```typescript
new Pty(transport: Transport, connectionConfig: ConnectionConfig): Pty

create(opts: PtyCreateOpts): Promise<CommandHandle>

kill(pid: number, opts?: Pick<ConnectionOpts, "requestTimeoutMs">): Promise<boolean>

resize(
   pid: number,
   size: object,
opts?: Pick<ConnectionOpts, "requestTimeoutMs">): Promise<void>
```

--------------------------------

### Execute Code in E2B Sandbox (JavaScript/TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/README.md

Demonstrates how to create a sandbox instance, run code within it, and retrieve the execution results using the E2B JavaScript SDK. It requires the @e2b/code-interpreter package.

```typescript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create()
await sandbox.runCode('x = 1')

const execution = await sandbox.runCode('x+=1; x')
console.log(execution.text)  // outputs 2
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Pty Module (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

Instantiates the Pty module, which is used for managing pseudo-terminals within the sandbox. It requires a transport layer and connection configuration.

```typescript
new Pty(transport: Transport, connectionConfig: ConnectionConfig): Pty
```

### Python: Get JavaScript Representation

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.0.3/sandbox/page.mdx

Returns the JavaScript representation of the data. This function can be used to generate or retrieve JavaScript code snippets.

```python
def _repr_javascript_() -> Optional[str]:
    # Returns the JavaScript representation of the data.
```

### E2B Pty: Constructor and Core Methods

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.2/commands/page.mdx

Initializes the Pty module and provides methods for creating new pseudo-terminals and killing existing ones. It requires a transport layer and connection configuration.

```typescript
new Pty(transport: Transport, connectionConfig: ConnectionConfig): Pty

create(opts: PtyCreateOpts): Promise<CommandHandle>

kill(pid: number, opts?: Pick<ConnectionOpts, "requestTimeoutMs">): Promise<boolean>
```

### CommandConnectOpts Type Alias - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.1.0/commands/page.mdx

Defines options for connecting to an existing command. This type alias combines settings for standard output and error callbacks, command timeout, and request timeouts, derived from `CommandStartOpts` and `CommandRequestOpts`.

```typescript
type CommandConnectOpts = Pick<CommandStartOpts, "onStderr" | "onStdout" | "timeoutMs"> & CommandRequestOpts;
```

--------------------------------

### Python Method for JavaScript Representation of Result

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/code-interpreter-python-sdk/v1.1.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Generates a JavaScript string representation of the Result object. Returns None if no JavaScript representation is available.

```python
def _repr_javascript_() -> Optional[str]

```

--------------------------------

### Fetch All Pages of Sandboxes (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/list/page.mdx

This JavaScript code illustrates how to fetch all available pages of sandboxes. It uses a while loop that continues as long as 'paginator.hasNext' is true, accumulating all sandbox items.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const paginator = Sandbox.list()

// Loop through all pages
const sandboxes: SandboxInfo[] = []
while (paginator.hasNext) { // $HighlightLine
  const items = await paginator.nextItems()
  sandboxes.push(...items)
}
```

--------------------------------

### CommandRequestOpts Interface

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.7.1/commands/page.mdx

Specifies options for making requests related to commands. This interface is extended by `CommandStartOpts` and primarily includes the `requestTimeoutMs` property for setting timeouts on API requests.

```typescript
interface CommandRequestOpts {
  requestTimeoutMs?: number;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Filter Sandboxes by Metadata (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/list/page.mdx

This JavaScript code filters sandboxes based on specific metadata key-value pairs defined during sandbox creation. It queries for sandboxes matching 'userId' and 'env'.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

// Create sandbox with metadata.
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create({
  metadata: {
    env: 'dev', // $HighlightLine
    app: 'my-app', // $HighlightLine
    userId: '123', // $HighlightLine
  },
})

// List all sandboxes that has `userId` key with value `123` and `env` key with value `dev`.
const paginator = Sandbox.list({
  query: {
    metadata: { userId: '123', env: 'dev' }, // $HighlightLine
  },
})

const sandboxes = await paginator.nextItems() // $HighlightLine
```

--------------------------------

### CommandConnectOpts Type Alias - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

The `CommandConnectOpts` type alias is a composite type used for options when connecting to an existing command. It combines settings for stderr/stdout callbacks and command timeout from `CommandStartOpts` with request timeout options from `CommandRequestOpts`.

```typescript
type CommandConnectOpts: Pick<CommandStartOpts, "onStderr" | "onStdout" | "timeoutMs"> & CommandRequestOpts;
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandboxes with Pagination (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sandbox/list/page.mdx

Demonstrates listing sandboxes using Sandbox.list() and retrieving paginated results in JavaScript. This method supports fetching sandboxes in pages. It imports necessary classes from '@e2b/code-interpreter'.

```javascript
import { Sandbox, SandboxInfo } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create(
  {
    metadata: {
      name: 'My Sandbox',
    },
  },
)

const paginator = Sandbox.list()

// Get the first page of sandboxes (running and paused)
const firstPage = await paginator.nextItems()

const runningSandbox = firstPage[0]

console.log('Running sandbox metadata:', runningSandbox.metadata)
console.log('Running sandbox id:', runningSandbox.sandboxId)
console.log('Running sandbox started at:', runningSandbox.startedAt)
console.log('Running sandbox template id:', runningSandbox.templateId)

// Get the next page of sandboxes
const nextPage = await paginator.nextItems()
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Run Command (Flexible Mode)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.10.0/commands/page.mdx

Starts a new command with flexible options. If opts.background is true, returns a CommandHandle for background execution. Otherwise, waits for completion and returns a CommandResult.

```typescript
run(cmd: string, opts?: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandResult | CommandHandle>
```

--------------------------------

### Define RateLimitError Class in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.4/errors/page.mdx

This TypeScript code defines the RateLimitError class, which inherits from SandboxError. It is thrown when an API's rate limits have been exceeded, preventing further requests. The constructor accepts a message argument.

```typescript
class RateLimitError extends SandboxError {
  constructor(message: any) {
    super(message);
  }
}

// Defined in errors.ts:92
```

--------------------------------

### Commands Constructor and Methods - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.1.1/commands/page.mdx

Initializes the Commands module and provides methods to manage commands within the sandbox. This includes starting, connecting to, listing, killing, and sending input to commands. It returns handles for interacting with command processes.

```typescript
new Commands(transport: Transport, connectionConfig: ConnectionConfig): Commands

connect(pid: number, opts?: CommandConnectOpts): Promise<CommandHandle>

kill(pid: number, opts?: CommandRequestOpts): Promise<boolean>

list(opts?: CommandRequestOpts): Promise<ProcessInfo[]>

run(cmd: string, opts?: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandResult>
run(cmd: string, opts?: CommandStartOpts & object): Promise<CommandHandle>

sendStdin(
   pid: number,
   data: string,
opts?: CommandRequestOpts): Promise<void>
```

--------------------------------

### Screenshot Functionality in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-js-sdk/v1.9.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Details the TypeScript method for capturing a screenshot within the sandbox environment. The `screenshot` function returns a Promise that resolves with a `Uint8Array` representing the image data (ArrayBufferLike).

```typescript
/**
 * Take a screenshot.
 * @returns A Promise that resolves with a Uint8Array representing the screenshot image data.
 */
screenshot(): Promise<Uint8Array<ArrayBufferLike>>;

```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Commands Module (TypeScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

Instantiates the Commands module, which is used for interacting with commands within the sandbox. It requires a transport layer and connection configuration.

```typescript
new Commands(transport: Transport, connectionConfig: ConnectionConfig): Commands
```

--------------------------------

### Start Custom Sandbox with Template ID (JavaScript)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/install-custom-packages/page.mdx

Starts a new sandbox instance using a previously built custom template. The template ID is passed to the `Sandbox.create` method in JavaScript.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sbx = Sandbox.create({
  template: 'YOUR_TEMPLATE_ID',
})
```

--------------------------------

### CommandStartOpts Interface - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.0.6/commands/page.mdx

The `CommandStartOpts` interface defines the configuration options for starting a new command. It includes settings for background execution, working directory, environment variables, callbacks for stdout/stderr, request timeouts, command timeouts, and the user to run the command as. Some properties have default values.

```typescript
interface CommandStartOpts {
  background?: boolean;
  cwd?: string;
  envs?: Record<string, string>;
  onStderr?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  onStdout?: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
  requestTimeoutMs?: number;
  timeoutMs?: number;
  user?: Username;
}
```

--------------------------------

### Start Command Options - TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.2.5/commands/page.mdx

Specifies options for starting a new command within the sandbox. This includes settings for background execution, working directory, environment variables, standard error/output callbacks, request timeouts, command-specific timeouts, and the user to run the command as. Defaults are provided for most options.

```typescript
optional background: boolean;
optional cwd: string;
optional envs: Record<string, string>;
optional onStderr: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
optional onStdout: (data: string) => void | Promise<void>;
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;
optional timeoutMs: number;
optional user: Username;
```
